chatbot/PLAN.md — Full implementation plan, gate inventory
Cost/Latency Funnel — L0 static rules run first
Cloud Tier (Optional) — AIRS Inlet + AIRS Dual
See docs/QUICKSTART.md for the full setup guide
Coverage matrix spans all pipeline gates
Both implement cheapest-first tiered decision funnels: L0<L1<L2<L3<L4<L5 and ALLOWLIST<PATH<REGEX<LLM
Gate ordering rationale — cheapest gates first, mod_llm always last
RAG Simulation — inject simulated retrieved document to test indirect prompt injection
deanonymize reads Vault populated by fast_scan to restore PII placeholders
PHI dataset — Names, DOBs, SSNs targeted by PII detection gate
LLM-as-judge tradeoff table explaining why mod_llm is used despite cost
PHI in RAG context would surface in model output
LLM tier — local Ollama guard model
bank_policy_poisoning is a concrete example of indirect injection via RAG
company_directory_backdoor is indirect injection via RAG document
helpdesk_faq_poisoning is indirect injection via RAG document
Claude Code hooks in .claude/settings.json, Gemini CLI hooks in .gemini/settings.json
Prerequisites: See Coding Agent Guard Setup in QUICKSTART.md
Both documents catalogue bypass techniques for different security subsystems in the same project
Chain 2 supply chain attack directly motivates Phase 5
Level 3 allowlist_enabled: false — force every call through guard model
Gap Summary table references hardening controls
Both are layered hardening playbooks (Level 1-4 vs threshold tuning guide) for different subsystems
Both describe multi-step attack sequences that evade individual per-call or per-gate checks
Container isolation mitigates multi-step staging attack impact
Injection Guard specifically targets prompt injection via tool output/input
Injection lineage tracks sleeping injections across agent boundaries
Mitigation: Pre-process tool_input with a base64/hex decode pass — directly implemented as agentic_arch_base64_defense
Shannon entropy detects encrypted/compressed exfiltration payloads
ALLOWLIST → PATH → REGEX → LLM decision priority
Phase 3 extends agentic_guard.py Write/Edit interception with linting
UI reads from audit JSONL files
Multi-Agent UI — Claude + Gemini agent column
Reasoning for Separation — workbench is diagnostic, Guard is production-grade
Section 1 Threat Framework Mapping — OWASP LLM Top 10
Indirect injection via RAG is primary real-world vector for classify gate
Chains combine bypass techniques from gate-by-gate analysis
Hardening builds on coverage gaps identified in matrix
## Design Principle: Stateless Gates, Stateful Pipeline
payload.raw_traces is a dict keyed by gate name
Iterates a list of payloads through PipelineManager.execute()
Used by pipeline running behind the scenes
The vault field is the bridge between FastScanGate and DeanonymizeGate
Always classifying original_input closes this bypass path
Key differentiator from mod_llm: Llama Guard 3 uses fixed taxonomy; Semantic Guard is domain-customizable
Runs before DeanonymizeGate so it compares the response the model actually produced

For runtime prompt/response security, see GATE-AIRS.md
AIRS-Inlet — scans the user prompt before it reaches the LLM
AIRS-Dual — scans the LLM response after generation

canary response captured → BehavioralAnalyzer reads it
